talk_metadata.talk_title: Mechanobiology of glioblastoma invasion using hyaluronic acid-based biomaterials
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we're under no illusion whatsoever that we can recapitulate all of the nuances of a patient tumor with this very simple in vitro system"
Speaker said: "we don't know what induces hyaluronidase secretion"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Limited cell numbers prevent omics-scale screening and may miss rare invasive subpopulations

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker detailed the chemistry (hydroxyl to methacrylate, Michael addition) suggesting the core scaffold is not novel and could be replicated
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Large-scale production of uniform HA hydrogels for high-throughput screening is challenging
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Focus on in vitro HA models without clear path to clinical biomarker validation

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that leader cells secrete HYAL2 to digest HA and facilitate invasion, then concluded that "we are still not sure what triggers hyaluronidase secretion" – contradiction between mechanistic insight and lack of upstream trigger

key_data_gaps: No in vivo validation of HA platform findings

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify transcriptional signatures of invasive rim cells that correlate with patient outcomes using CrisPRO's spatial transcriptomics
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted matching hits between patient GBM invasive rim nanoString data and in vitro HA platform
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate bulk and spatial data to pinpoint invasive biomarkers for patient stratification
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: search for GBM invasion biomarker studies

crispro_opportunity.description: Target the transsulfuration pathway (cysteine/glutathione synthesis) identified as essential for invasive GBM cells
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker reported that pharmacologic or genetic inhibition of the transsulfuration pathway limits invasion
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model metabolic dependencies and predict synergistic drug combos with existing GBM therapies
external_validation_needed: Check FDA pipeline for transsulfuration inhibitors
Assess preclinical efficacy in GBM PDX models

cited_competitors.name: Manish Aghi
cited_competitors.context: collaborator providing patient GBM biopsies for comparative analysis

unresolved_questions: What upstream signals trigger HYAL2 secretion by leader cells?
Can the HA invasion platform be scaled for high-throughput drug screening?

companies_to_monitor: Companies developing HA-based drug delivery or ECM-modulating agents

talk_metadata.talk_title: Stiffness-driven EMT via TYK2–Twist signaling in triple‑negative breast cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We don't know what the sensor is"
Speaker said: "We have no phenotype when we block JAK1/2 or interferon signaling, only TYK2 inhibition shows effect"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: TYK2 acts as a metastasis suppressor; inhibition paradoxically enhances invasion via Twist nuclear translocation
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker admitted: "We don't know why TYK2 inhibition increases invasion"
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: In soft matrix conditions, some cells become highly invasive after TYK2 inhibition

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: NCT04165772 (hypothetical TYK2 inhibitor trial in breast cancer)
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: TYK2 inhibition to modulate inflammation in TNBC
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling patients regardless of tumor stiffness or EMT status, potentially diluting signal
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Tumor matrix stiffness or Twist nuclear localization as predictive biomarker
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Primary endpoint based on tumor shrinkage may miss metastasis‑promotion effect

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker highlighted that TYK2 inhibition has off‑target pro‑invasive effects, suggesting limited therapeutic window
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Pursuing TYK2 inhibition without stratifying patients by matrix stiffness may lead to adverse outcomes

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that stiff matrices activate Lin kinase to phosphorylate Twist and drive EMT, then concluded that "stiffness alone is sufficient" without acknowledging the parallel finding that TYK2 loss also drives Twist nuclear translocation independent of stiffness

key_data_gaps: Lack of patient stratification by tumor stiffness
No mechanistic link between TYK2 membrane dissociation and upstream sensor

crispro_opportunity.description: Target the TYK2–Lin–Twist axis to prevent stiffness‑induced EMT and invasion
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker demonstrated that TYK2 inhibition increases Twist nuclear localization and invasion in soft matrices
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model the signaling network and predict combination therapies (e.g., Lin inhibitors + TYK2 stabilizers) to block EMT
external_validation_needed: PubMed: studies on TYK2 role in breast cancer metastasis
ClinicalTrials.gov: check ongoing TYK2 inhibitor trials

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a stiffness‑based biomarker (e.g., membrane vs cytosolic TYK2 localization) to select patients for TYK2‑targeted therapies
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker showed loss of membrane TYK2 in invasive breast cancer tissue microarrays
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate imaging‑derived stiffness metrics with proteomic data to create a predictive classifier
external_validation_needed: Human Protein Atlas: TYK2 localization data

cited_competitors.name: JAK1/2 inhibitors (e.g., ruxolitinib)
cited_competitors.context: Compared to TYK2 inhibitors, JAK1/2 inhibitors showed no invasive phenotype

cited_competitors.name: Lin kinase inhibitors
cited_competitors.context: Used to block Twist phosphorylation and invasion

unresolved_questions: What is the upstream mechanosensor that causes TYK2 membrane dissociation under stiffness?
Can targeting Lin kinase fully suppress stiffness‑induced EMT without affecting normal tissue homeostasis?
